chapter_title.en: Chapter 87 – Limit of the breast-feeding which (makes marriage to be) Prohibited

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/207)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/207)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْغُلَامِ يَرْضَعُ الرَّضْعَةَ وَ الرَّضْعَتَيْنِ فَقَالَ لَا يُحَرِّمُ فَعَدَدْتُ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى أَكْمَلْتُ عَشْرَ رَضَعَاتٍ فَقَالَ إِذَا كَانَتْ مُتَفَرِّقَةً [فَلَا] .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Fazzal, from Abdullah Bin Sinan, from Umar Bin Yazeed who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the boy who is breast-fed the breast-feeding session, and the two breast-feeding sessions. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It does not make a Prohibition’. So I counted to him until I completed ten breast-feeding sessions. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If it was separated, so no’.
en.sarwar: 8. Al-Husayn from Muhammad has narrated from Mu’alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali ibn Faddal from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan from ‘Umar ibn Yazid who has said the following: “This is concerning my question before abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a boy who is breastfed once and twice. He (the Imam) said, ‘It does not cause unlawfulness.’ I counted up to ten times breastfeeding. He (the Imam) said, ‘If they are on different times they do not cause unlawfulness.’”
